course_description:
This course covers advanced topics in algebra and number theory, building on third year courses such as abstract algebra and complex analysis. This course will be crucial for those doing Honours study in pure mathematics, especially those interested in algebra and number theory.

This is an advanced course in Algebra and Number Theory intended for high level undergraduate or Honours students.

timetable:

course_aims: The aim of this course is to develop notions and structures in algebra and number theory and to use these to analyse a selection of advanced topics in the area.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain and interpret concepts in analytic and algebraic number theory
Solve problems and prove results in analytic and algebraic number theory
Evaluate important ideas in the field of abstract algebra
Solve problems and prove results in abstract algebra

assessment_summary.title: Oral exam
assessment_summary.due_date: Exam week 1 Mon - Exam week 2 Fri

assessment_details.title: In-Semester Quiz
assessment_details.task_description: In the In-Semester Quiz, students will be asked to answer questions on the material covered in the first 6 weeks of lectures. Students who miss the Quiz have to do an oral in-semester test instead, covering the same material. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This is an in-class quiz.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Students who miss the written in-class quiz will have to do the quiz orally.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: 10-page report plus oral presentation
assessment_details.task_description: Students are expected to write a 10-page report on a topic in algebra & representation theory and give a 20-minute talk on their report. All talks will be held in the final three weeks of semester. The report will be worth 20% and the talk 20% for a combined 40%. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Gradescope will be used for the submission of the reports
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Presentation following the assessment report will be 7 days after the assessment is due and as such, an extension after 7 days will not be possible.
Late submission
A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Oral exam
assessment_details.due_date: Exam week 1 Mon - Exam week 2 Fri
assessment_details.task_description: There will be a 45-minute oral final exam. Students may use the board, or pen and paper, to assist in answering questions. Read full task description Hide full task description
